member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder
member.summary: Used to build a CSP header from multiple directives.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddDefaultSrc
member.summary: The default-src directive serves as a fallback for the other CSP fetch directives. Valid sources include 'self', 'unsafe-inline', 'unsafe-eval', 'none', scheme such as http:, or internet hosts by name or IP address, as well as an optional URL scheme and/or port number. The site's address may include an optional leading wildcard (the asterisk character, '*'), and you may use a wildcard (again, '*') as the port number, indicating that all legal ports are valid for the source.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddChildSrc
member.summary: The child directive specifies valid sources for web workers and nested browsing contexts loaded using elements such as <frame> and <iframe> For workers, non-compliant requests are treated as fatal network errors by the user agent.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddConnectSrc
member.summary: The connect-src directive restricts the URLs which can be loaded using script interfaces The APIs that are restricted are: <a> ping, Fetch, XMLHttpRequest, WebSocket, and EventSource.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddFontSrc
member.summary: The font-src directive specifies valid sources for fonts loaded using @font-face.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddObjectSrc
member.summary: The object-src directive specifies valid sources for the <object>, <embed>, and <applet> elements
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddFormAction
member.summary: The form-action directive restricts the URLs which can be used as the target of a form submissions from a given context
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddImgSrc
member.summary: The img-src directive specifies valid sources of images and favicons>

member.summary: The script-src directive specifies valid sources for sources for JavaScript.

member.summary: The script-src-attr directive specifies valid sources for JavaScript inline event handlers.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddScriptSrcElem
member.summary: The script-src-elem directive specifies valid sources for JavaScript <script> elements.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddStyleSrc
member.summary: The style-src directive specifies valid sources for sources for stylesheets.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddStyleSrcAttr
member.summary: The style-src-attr directive specifies valid sources for inline styles applied to individual DOM elements.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddStyleSrcElem
member.summary: The style-src-elem directive specifies valid sources for stylesheet <style> elements and <link> elements with rel="stylesheet".
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddMediaSrc
member.summary: The media-src directive specifies valid sources for loading media using the <audio> and <video> elements
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddManifestSrc
member.summary: The manifest-src directive specifies which manifest can be applied to the resource.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddFrameAncestors
member.summary: The frame-ancestors directive specifies valid parents that may embed a page using <frame>, <iframe>, <object>, <embed>, or <applet>. Setting this directive to 'none' is similar to X-Frame-Options: DENY (which is also supported in older browsers).
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddFrameSrc
member.summary: The frame-src directive specifies valid sources for nested browsing contexts loading using elements such as <frame> and <iframe>
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddWorkerSrc
member.summary: The worker-src directive specifies valid sources for Worker, SharedWorker, or ServiceWorker scripts.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddBaseUri
member.summary: The base-uri directive restricts the URLs which can be used in a document's <base> element. If this value is absent, then any URI is allowed. If this directive is absent, the user agent will use the value in the <base> element.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddSandbox
member.summary: The sandbox directive enables a sandbox for the requested resource similar to the <script> sandbox attribute. It applies restrictions to a page's actions including preventing popups, preventing the execution of plugins and scripts, and enforcing a same-origin policy.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddUpgradeInsecureRequests
member.summary: The upgrade-insecure-requests directive instructs user agents to treat all of a site's insecure URLs (those served over HTTP) as though they have been replaced with secure URLs (those served over HTTPS). This directive is intended for web sites with large numbers of insecure legacy URLs that need to be rewritten.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddBlockAllMixedContent
member.summary:
The block-all-mixed-content directive prevents loading any assets using HTTP when the page is loaded using HTTPS.
All mixed content resource requests are blocked, including both active and passive mixed content. This also applies to <iframe> documents, ensuring the entire page is mixed content free.
The upgrade-insecure-requests directive is evaluated before block-all-mixed-content and If the former is set, the latter is effectively a no-op. It is recommended to set one directive or the other – not both.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddReportUri
member.summary: The report-uri directive instructs the user agent to report attempts to violate the Content Security Policy. These violation reports consist of JSON documents sent via an HTTP POST request to the specified URI.
member.returns: A configured
member.remarks: NOTE: this directive has been deprecated in favour of Report-To. Use instead.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddReportTo(System.String)
member.summary: The report-to directive instructs the user agent to send requests to an endpoint defined in a Report-To HTTP header. The directive has no effect in and of itself, but only gains meaning in combination with other reporting directives.
member.param: The name of the group in the Report-To JSON field to send reports to
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddRequireTrustedTypesFor
member.summary:
The require-trusted-types-for directive instructs user agents to control the data passed to DOM XSS sink functions, like Element.innerHTML setter.
When used, those functions only accept non-spoofable, typed values created by Trusted Type policies, and reject strings. Together with which guards creation of Trusted Type policies, this allows authors to define rules guarding writing values to the DOM and thus reducing the DOM XSS attack surface to small, isolated parts of the web application codebase, facilitating their monitoring and code review.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CspBuilder.AddTrustedTypes
member.summary:
The trusted-types directive instructs user agents to restrict the creation of Trusted Types policies - functions that build non-spoofable, typed values intended to be passed to DOM XSS sinks in place of strings.
Together with this allows authors to define rules guarding writing values to the DOM and thus reducing the DOM XSS attack surface to small, isolated parts of the web application codebase, facilitating their monitoring and code review. This directive declares an allowlist of trusted type policy names created with trustedTypes.createPolicy from Trusted Types API.
member.returns: A configured

member.summary: Create a custom CSP directive for an un-implemented directive
member.param: The directive name, e.g. default-src

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginEmbedderPolicyBuilder
member.summary: Used to build a Cross Origin Embedder Policy header from multiple directives.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginEmbedderPolicyBuilder.UnsafeNone
member.summary: This is the default value. Allows the document to fetch cross-origin resources without giving explicit permission through the CORS protocol or the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header. From: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy#directives
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginEmbedderPolicyBuilder.RequireCorp
member.summary: A document can only load resources from the same origin, or resources explicitly marked as loadable from another origin. If a cross origin resource supports CORS, the crossorigin attribute or the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header must be used to load it without being blocked by COEP. From: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy#directives
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginEmbedderPolicyBuilder.Credentialless
member.summary: no-cors cross-origin requests are sent without credentials. In particular, it means Cookies are omitted from the request, and ignored from the response. The responses are allowed without an explicit permission via the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header. Navigate responses behave similarly as the require-corp mode: They require Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy response header. From: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy#directives
member.returns: A configured

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginOpenerPolicyBuilder
member.summary: Used to build a Cross Origin Opener Policy header from multiple directives.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginOpenerPolicyBuilder.UnsafeNone
member.summary: This is the default value. Allows the document to be added to its opener's browsing context group unless the opener itself has a COOP of same-origin or same-origin-allow-popups. From: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy#directives
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginOpenerPolicyBuilder.SameOrigin
member.summary: Isolates the browsing context exclusively to same-origin documents. Cross-origin documents are not loaded in the same browsing context. From: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy#directives
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginOpenerPolicyBuilder.SameOriginAllowPopups
member.summary: Retains references to newly opened windows or tabs which either don't set COOP or which opt out of isolation by setting a COOP of unsafe-none. From: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy#directives
member.returns: A configured

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginResourcePolicyBuilder

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginResourcePolicyBuilder.SameOrigin

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.CrossOriginResourcePolicyBuilder.CrossOrigin

Specifies the URI to which the user agent should report Expect-CT failures.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.ExpectCTHeaderExtensions.AddExpectCT(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection,System.Int32,System.String,System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Add Expect-CT max-age= to all HTTPS requests. Allows sites to opt in to reporting and/or enforcement of Certificate Transparency requirements. Tells the user-agent to cache the domain in the Expect-CT list for the number of seconds provided.
member.param: The collection of policies
Specifies the number of seconds after reception of the Expect-CT header field during which the user agent should regard the host from whom the message was received as a known Expect-CT host.
Specifies the URI to which the user agent should report Expect-CT failures.

If true, signals to the user agent that compliance with the Certificate Transparency policy should be enforced (rather than only reporting compliance) and that the user agent should refuse future connections that violate its Certificate Transparency policy.
A collection of host names that will not add the Expect-CT header
member.remarks: When both the enforce directive and the report-uri directive are present, the configuration is referred to as an "enforce-and-report" configuration, signalling to the user agent both that compliance to the Certificate Transparency policy should be enforced and that violations should be reported.
member.returns: The for method chaining

Configure the Permissions-Policy

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyHeaderExtensions.AddPermissionsPolicyWithDefaultSecureDirectives(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection)
member.summary: Add a Permissions-Policy with recommended "secure" directives based on OWASP recommendations. Note that this disables many features. If you need to enable some features, consider calling and , and overriding one or more policies.
member.param: The collection of policies
member.returns: The for method chaining
member.remarks:
The OWASP recommended policy includes directives that are either experimental, not available by default, or not implemented. For consistency with , those directives are not included in the policy.
The policy added is equivalent to accelerometer=(), autoplay=(), camera=(), display-capture=(), encrypted-media=(), fullscreen=(), geolocation=(), gyroscope=(), magnetometer=(), microphone=(), midi=(), payment=(), picture-in-picture=(), publickey-credentials-get=(), screen-wake-lock=(), sync-xhr=(), usb=(), web-share=(), xr-spatial-tracking=()

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder
member.summary: Used for building a Permissions-Policy header

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddAccelerometer
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the accelerometer sensor. If disabled then constructing of a Sensor-based interface object will throw a SecurityError. The events are not fired. If an interface (or an event) requires access to multiple sensors of different types than each of the corresponding sensor features must be allowed in order to use the interface.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddAttributionReporting
member.summary:
The HTTP Permissions-Policy header attribution-reporting directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Attribution Reporting API. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks the use of this feature:
Background attributionsrc requests won't be made.
The XMLHttpRequest.setAttributionReporting() method will throw an exception when called.
The attributionReporting option, when included on a fetch() call, will cause it to throw an exception.
Registration headers (Attribution-Reporting-Register-Source and Attribution-Reporting-Register-Trigger) in HTTP responses on associated documents will be ignored.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddAmbientLightSensor
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the ambient light sensor sensor. If disabled then constructing of a Sensor-based interface object will throw a SecurityError. The events are not fired. If an interface (or an event) requires access to multiple sensors of different types than each of the corresponding sensor features must be allowed in order to use the interface.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddAutoplay
member.summary: Controls access to autoplay of media requested through the HTMLMediaElement interface. If disabled in a document, then calls to play() without a user gesture will reject the promise with a NotAllowedError DOMException object as its parameter. The autoplay attribute will be ignored.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddBluetooth
member.summary:
The HTTP Permissions-Policy header bluetooth directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Web Bluetooth API. Specifically, where a defined policy disallows use of this feature, the methods of the Bluetooth object returned by Navigator.bluetooth, will block access:
Bluetooth.getAvailability() will always fulfill its returned Promise with a value of false.
Bluetooth.getDevices() will reject its returned Promise with a SecurityError DOMException.
Bluetooth.requestDevice() will reject its returned Promise with a SecurityError DOMException.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddCamera
member.summary: Controls access to video input devices requested through the NavigatorUserMedia interface. If disabled in a document, then calls to getUserMedia() will not grant access to video input devices in that document.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddDisplayCapture
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header display-capture directive controls whether or not the document is permitted to use Screen Capture API, that is, getDisplayMedia() to capture the screen's contents. If display-capture is disabled in a document, the document will not be able to initiate screen capture via getDisplayMedia() and will throw a NotAllowedError exception.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddEncryptedMedia
member.summary: Controls whether encrypted media extensions are available. If disabled The promise returned by requestMediaKeySystemAccess() must return a promise which rejects with a SecurityError DOMException object as its parameter.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddFullscreen
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use Element.requestFullScreen(). When this policy is enabled, the returned Promise rejects with a TypeError.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddGeolocation
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Geolocation Interface. When this policy is enabled, calls to getCurrentPosition() and watchPosition() will cause those functions' callbacks to be invoked with a PositionError code of PERMISSION_DENIED.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddGyroscope
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the gyroscope sensor. If disabled then constructing of a Sensor-based interface object will throw a SecurityError. The events are not fired. If an interface (or an event) requires access to multiple sensors of different types than each of the corresponding sensor features must be allowed in order to use the interface.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddFederatedLearningOfCohortsCalculation
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to make a FLoC calculation. If disabled in a document, the promise returned by interestCohort() must reject with a DOMException parameter.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddHid
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header hid directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the WebHID API to connect to uncommon or exotic human interface devices such as alternative keyboards or gamepads. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks WebHID usage, the Navigator.hid property will not be available.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddIdentityCredentialsGet
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header identity-credentials-get directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Federated Credential Management API (FedCM), and more specifically the navigator.credentials.get() method with an identity option. Where this policy forbids use of the API, the Promise returned by the get() call will reject with a NotAllowedError DOMException.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddIdleDetection
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header idle-detection directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Idle Detection API to detect when users are interacting with their devices, for example to report "available"/"away" status in chat applications. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, IdleDetector.start() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type NotAllowedError.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddLocalFonts
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header local-fonts directive controls whether the current document is allowed to gather data on the user's locally-installed fonts via the Window.queryLocalFonts() method. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, Promises returned by Window.queryLocalFonts() will reject with a DOMException of type SecurityError.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddMagnetometer
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the magnetometer sensor. If disabled then constructing of a Sensor-based interface object will throw a SecurityError. The events are not fired. If an interface (or an event) requires access to multiple sensors of different types than each of the corresponding sensor features must be allowed in order to use the interface.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddMicrophone
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use audio input devices. When this policy is enabled, the Promise returned by MediaDevices.getUserMedia() will reject with a NotAllowedError.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddMidi
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Web MIDI API. If disabled in a document, the promise returned by requestMIDIAccess() must reject with a DOMException parameter.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddOtpCredentials
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header otp-credentials directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the WebOTP API to request a one-time password (OTP) from a specially-formatted SMS message sent by the app's server, i.e., via navigator.credentials.get({otp: ..., ...}). Specifically, where a defined policy blocks the use of this feature, the Promise returned by navigator.credentials.get({otp}) will reject with a SecurityError DOMException.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddPayment
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the PaymentRequest interface. If disabled then calls to the PaymentRequest constuctor will throw a SecurityError.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddPictureInPicture
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use Picture In Picture. If disabled in a document, then calls to requestPictureInPicture() will throw a SecurityError and pictureInPictureEnabled will return false.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddPublickeyCredentialsCreate
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header publickey-credentials-create directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Web Authentication API to create new WebAuthn credentials, i.e., via navigator.credentials.create({publicKey}). Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, the Promise returned by navigator.credentials.create({publicKey}) will reject with a NotAllowedError DOMException. If the method is called cross-origin. the Promise will also reject with a NotAllowedError if the feature is granted by allow= on an iframe and the frame does not also have Transient activation.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddPublickeyCredentialsGet
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header publickey-credentials-get directive controls whether the current document is allowed to access the Web Authentication API to retrieve public-key credentials, i.e., via navigator.credentials.get({publicKey}). Specifically, where a defined policy blocks the use of this feature, the Promise returned by navigator.credentials.get({publicKey}) will reject with a NotAllowedError DOMException.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddScreenWakeLock
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header screen-wake-lock directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use Screen Wake Lock API to indicate that the device should not dim or turn off the screen. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, WakeLock.request() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type NotAllowedError.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddSerialPermissions
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header serial directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Web Serial API to communicate with serial devices, either directly connected via a serial port, or via USB or Bluetooth devices emulating a serial port. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, Serial.requestPort() and Serial.getPorts() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type SecurityError.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddSpeaker
member.summary: Controls access to audio output devices requested through the NavigatorUserMedia interface. If disabled then calls to getUserMedia() will not grant access to audio output devices in that document.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddSpeakerSelection
member.summary:
The HTTP Permissions-Policy header speaker-selection directive controls whether the current document is allowed to enumerate and select audio output devices (speakers, headphones, and so on). Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature:
MediaDevices.enumerateDevices() won't return devices of type audio output.
MediaDevices.selectAudioOutput() won't display the popup for selecting an audio output, and the returned Promise will reject with a DOMException of type NotAllowedError.
HTMLMediaElement.setSinkId() and AudioContext.setSinkId() will throw a NotAllowedError if called for an audio output.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddStorageAccess
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header storage-access directive controls whether a document loaded in a third-party context (i.e. embedded in an iframe) is allowed to use the Storage Access API to request access to unpartitioned cookies. This is relevant to user agents that by default block access to unpartitioned cookies by sites loaded in a third-party context to improve privacy (for example, to prevent tracking). Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, Document.requestStorageAccess() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type NotAllowedError.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddSyncXHR
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use synchronous XMLHttpRequest transfers. If disabled in a document, then calls to send() on XMLHttpRequest objects will throw a NetworkError.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddUsb
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the WebUSB API. If disabled then calls to getDevices() should return a promise which rejects with a SecurityError DOMException.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddVR
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the WebVR API. If disabled then calls to getVRDisplays() should return a promise which rejects with a SecurityError DOMException.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddWebShare
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header web-share directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Navigator.share() method of the Web Share API to share text, links, images, and other content to arbitrary destinations of the user's choice. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks usage of this feature, Navigator.share() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type NotAllowedError.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddWindowManagement
member.summary:
The HTTP Permissions-Policy header window-management directive controls whether or not the current document is allowed to use the Window Management API to manage windows on multiple displays. Where this policy forbids use of the API:
The Promise returned by the Window.getScreenDetails() method will reject with a NotAllowedError exception.
The Window.screen.isExtended property will always return false.
member.returns: A configured

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.PermissionsPolicyBuilder.AddXrSpatialTracking
member.summary:
The HTTP Permissions-Policy header xr-spatial-tracking directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the WebXR Device API. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks usage of this feature:
navigator.xr.isSessionSupported() and navigator.xr.requestSession() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type SecurityError.
devicechange events are not fired on the navigator.xr object.
member.returns: A configured

Create a custom Permissions-Policy directive for an un-implemented directive.
The directive can be built using standard methods such as and

member.summary: Indicates that the site doesn't want to set a Referrer Policy here and the browser should fallback to a Referrer Policy defined via other mechanisms elsewhere
member.param: The collection of policies
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.ReferrerPolicyHeaderExtensions.AddReferrerPolicyNoReferrer(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection)
member.summary: Instructs the browser to never send the referrer header with requests that are made from your site. This also include links to pages on your own site.
member.param: The collection of policies
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.ReferrerPolicyHeaderExtensions.AddReferrerPolicyNoReferrerWhenDowngrade(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection)
member.summary: The browser will not send the referrer header when navigating from HTTPS to HTTP, but will always send the full URL in the referrer header when navigating from HTTP to any origin.
member.param: The collection of policies
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.ReferrerPolicyHeaderExtensions.AddReferrerPolicySameOrigin(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection)
member.summary: The browser will only set the referrer header on requests to the same origin. If the destination is another origin then no referrer information will be sent.
member.param: The collection of policies
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.ReferrerPolicyHeaderExtensions.AddReferrerPolicyOrigin(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection)
member.summary: The browser will always set the referrer header to the origin from which the request was made. This will strip any path information from the referrer information.
member.param: The collection of policies
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.ReferrerPolicyHeaderExtensions.AddReferrerPolicyStrictOrigin(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection)
member.summary: The browser will always set the referrer header to the origin from which the request was made, as long as the destination is HTTPS, otherwise no refer will not be sent. This will strip any path information from the referrer information.
member.param: The collection of policies
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.ReferrerPolicyHeaderExtensions.AddReferrerPolicyOriginWhenCrossOrigin(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection)
member.summary: The browser will send the full URL to requests to the same origin but only send the origin when requests are cross-origin.
member.param: The collection of policies
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.ReferrerPolicyHeaderExtensions.AddReferrerPolicyStrictOriginWhenCrossOrigin(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection)
member.summary: The browser will send the full URL to requests to the same origin but only send the origin when requests are cross-origin, as long as a scheme downgrade has not happened (i.e. you are not moving from HTTPS to HTTP)
member.param: The collection of policies
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.ReferrerPolicyHeaderExtensions.AddReferrerPolicyUnsafeUrl(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection)
member.summary: The browser will always send the full URL with any request to any origin.
member.param: The collection of policies
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.StrictTransportSecurityHeaderExtensions.AddStrictTransportSecurityMaxAgeIncludeSubDomainsAndPreload(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.HeaderPolicyCollection,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Add Strict-Transport-Security max-age=; includeSubDomains to all requests. Tells the user-agent to cache the domain in the STS list for the number of seconds provided and include any sub-domains. Additionally, enable preloading of the site in the HSTS preload list
WARNING:Sending the preload directive from your site can have PERMANENT CONSEQUENCES and prevent users from accessing your site and any of its subdomains if you find you need to switch back to HTTP.
member.param: The collection of policies
The maximum number of seconds to cache the domain
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.summary: Add Strict-Transport-Security to all HTTPS requests. Tells the user-agent to cache the domain in the STS list for the number of seconds provided.
member.param: The collection of policies
The maximum number of seconds to cache the domain
If true, includes sub-domains in the STS list

If true, enable preloading of the site in the HSTS preload list WARNING:Sending the preload directive from your site can have PERMANENT CONSEQUENCES and prevent users from accessing your site and any of its subdomains if you find you need to switch back to HTTP.
A collection of host names that will not add the HSTS header
member.returns: The for method chaining

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.BlockAllMixedContentDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The block-all-mixed-content directive prevents loading any assets using HTTP when the page is loaded using HTTPS.
All mixed content resource requests are blocked, including both active and passive mixed content. This also applies to <iframe> documents, ensuring the entire page is mixed content free.
The upgrade-insecure-requests directive is evaluated before block-all-mixed-content and If the former is set, the latter is effectively a no-op. It is recommended to set one directive or the other – not both.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.CustomDirectiveBuilder.UnsafeInline
member.summary: Allows the use of inline resources, such as inline <scripT> elements, javascript : URLs, inline event handlers, and inline <style> elements WARNING: This source is insecure - you should not use this directive if at all possible
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.CustomDirectiveBuilder.UnsafeHashes
member.summary: Allows enabling specific inline event handlers. If you only need to allow inline event handlers and not inline <script> elements or javascript: URLs, this is a safer method than using the unsafe-inline expression. WARNING: This source is insecure - you should not use this directive if at all possible
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.CustomDirectiveBuilder.UnsafeEval
member.summary: Allows the use of eval() and similar methods for creating code from strings. WARNING: This source is insecure - you should not use this directive if at all possible
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.CustomDirectiveBuilder.WasmUnsafeEval
member.summary: Allows the loading and execution of WebAssembly modules without the need to also allow unsafe JavaScript execution via 'unsafe-eval'. WARNING: This source is insecure - you should not use this directive if at all possible
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.CustomDirectiveBuilder.StrictDynamic
member.summary: The strict-dynamic source expression specifies that the trust explicitly given to a script present in the markup, by accompanying it with a nonce or a hash, shall be propagated to all the scripts loaded by that root script. At the same time, any whitelist or source expressions such as 'self' or 'unsafe-inline' will be ignored.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.CustomDirectiveBuilder.InlineSpeculationRules
member.summary: Allows the browser to load inline <script> elements that have a type attribute of speculationrules.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.CustomDirectiveBuilder.WithNonce
member.summary: A whitelist for specific inline scripts using a cryptographic nonce (number used once). The server generates a unique nonce value for each request. Specifying a nonce makes a modern browser ignore 'unsafe-inline' which could still be set for older browsers without nonce support.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.CustomDirectiveBuilder.ReportSample
member.summary: Requires a sample of the violating code to be included in the violation report
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.DefaultSourceDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The default-src directive serves as a fallback for the other CSP fetch directives. Valid sources include 'self', 'unsafe-inline', 'unsafe-eval', 'none', scheme such as http:, or internet hosts by name or IP address, as well as an optional URL scheme and/or port number. The site's address may include an optional leading wildcard (the asterisk character, '*'), and you may use a wildcard (again, '*') as the port number, indicating that all legal ports are valid for the source.

member.summary: Marker attribute used by the Content Security Policy source generator. Applying this attribute to a directive builder class instructs the generator to include the specified mixin helpers (e.g. support for 'self', host/scheme sources, nonce-, hashes, 'unsafe-*' tokens, etc.).

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.CspMixinAttribute.#ctor(NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Flags indicating which mixin helpers should be generated for the decorated class. Combine values from to enable multiple helpers.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes
member.summary: Used by source generator to generate mixins for CSP headers

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.Self
member.summary: Adds the 'self' source, allowing resources from the same origin.

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.HostSource
member.summary: Adds one or more host sources (e.g. https://cdn.example.com).

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.SchemeSource
member.summary: Adds scheme sources (e.g. https:, data:, blob:).

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.UnsafeEval
member.summary: Adds the 'unsafe-eval' source to permit eval-like constructs.

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.UnsafeInline
member.summary: Adds the 'unsafe-inline' source to permit inline scripts/styles.

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.UnsafeHashes
member.summary: Adds the 'unsafe-hashes' source to allow inline event handlers when accompanied by valid hashes.

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.Nonce
member.summary: Adds a nonce-... source to allow nonced scripts/styles.

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.Hash
member.summary: Adds hash sources (e.g. sha256-..., sha384-..., sha512-...).

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.ReportSample
member.summary: Adds the 'report-sample' token to include code samples in violation reports.

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.WasmUnsafeEval
member.summary: Adds the 'wasm-unsafe-eval' token to permit WebAssembly eval-like behavior.

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.InlineSpeculationRules
member.summary: Adds the 'inline-speculation-rules' token to allow inline speculation rules.

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.StrictDynamic
member.summary: Adds the 'strict-dynamic' token to trust dynamically added scripts when a nonce or hash is present.

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.None
member.summary: Adds the 'none' source, blocking all sources for the directive.

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.ScriptHashTagHelper
member.summary: Adds the 'WithHashTagHelper' source, by calling GetScriptCSPHashes

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.StyleHashTagHelper
member.summary: Adds the 'WithHashTagHelper' source, by calling GetStyleCSPHashes

member.name: F:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.MixinTypes.All
member.summary: Convenience combination of all mixin flags except and

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.ReportUriDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The report-uri directive instructs the user agent to report attempts to violate the Content Security Policy. These violation reports consist of JSON documents sent via an HTTP POST request to the specified URI.
member.remarks: NOTE: this directive has been deprecated in favour of Report-To. Use instead.

member.returns: Nothing, as can't be chained

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.RequireTrustedTypesForDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The require-trusted-types-for directive instructs user agents to control the data passed to DOM XSS sink functions, like Element.innerHTML setter.
When used, those functions only accept non-spoofable, typed values created by Trusted Type policies, and reject strings. Together with which guards creation of Trusted Type policies, this allows authors to define rules guarding writing values to the DOM and thus reducing the DOM XSS attack surface to small, isolated parts of the web application codebase, facilitating their monitoring and code review.

member.summary: Mark the script sink group as requiring trusted values

member.param: The sink group to add

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.SandboxDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The sandbox directive enables a sandbox for the requested resource similar to the <script> sandbox attribute. It applies restrictions to a page's actions including preventing popups, preventing the execution of plugins and scripts, and enforcing a same-origin policy.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: The sandbox tokens to apply.

member.summary: Allows for downloads after the user clicks a button or link.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.SandboxDirectiveBuilder.AllowForms
member.summary: Allows the page to submit forms. If this keyword is not used, this operation is not allowed.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.SandboxDirectiveBuilder.AllowModals
member.summary: Allows the page to open modal windows.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.summary: Allows the page to disable the ability to lock the screen orientation.

member.summary: Allows the page to use the Pointer Lock API.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.SandboxDirectiveBuilder.AllowPopups
member.summary: Allows popups (like from window.open, target="_blank", showModalDialog). If this keyword is not used, that functionality will silently fail.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.SandboxDirectiveBuilder.AllowPopupsToEscapeSandbox
member.summary: Allows a sandboxed document to open new windows without forcing the sandboxing flags upon them. This will allow, for example, a third-party advertisement to be safely sandboxed without forcing the same restrictions upon the page the ad links to.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.SandboxDirectiveBuilder.AllowPresentation
member.summary: Allows embedders to have control over whether an iframe can start a presentation session.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.SandboxDirectiveBuilder.AllowSameOrigin
member.summary: Allows the content to be treated as being from its normal origin. If this keyword is not used, the embedded content is treated as being from a unique origin.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.SandboxDirectiveBuilder.AllowScripts
member.summary: Allows the page to run scripts (but not create pop-up windows). If this keyword is not used, this operation is not allowed.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.summary: Allows the page to navigate (load) content to the top-level browsing context. If this keyword is not used, this operation is not allowed.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.ScriptSourceAttrDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The script-src-attr directive specifies valid sources for JavaScript inline event handlers. This directive only specifies valid sources for inline script event handlers like onclick.
It does not apply to other JavaScript sources that can trigger script execution, such as URLs loaded directly into <script> elements and XSLT stylesheets. (Valid sources can be specified for all JavaScript script sources using script-src, or just for <script> elements using script-src-elem.)

member.summary: Allows enabling specific inline event handlers. member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.ScriptSourceDirectiveBuilder.WasmUnsafeEval
member.summary: Allows the loading and execution of WebAssembly modules without the need to also allow unsafe JavaScript execution via 'unsafe-eval'. WARNING: This source is insecure - you should not use this directive if at all possible
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.ScriptSourceDirectiveBuilder.StrictDynamic
member.summary: The strict-dynamic source expression specifies that the trust explicitly given to a script present in the markup, by accompanying it with a nonce or a hash, shall be propagated to all the scripts loaded by that root script. At the same time, any whitelist or source expressions such as 'self' or 'unsafe-inline' will be ignored.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.ScriptSourceDirectiveBuilder.InlineSpeculationRules
member.summary: Allows the browser to load inline <script> elements that have a type attribute of speculationrules.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.ScriptSourceDirectiveBuilder.WithNonce
member.summary: A whitelist for specific inline scripts using a cryptographic nonce (number used once). The server generates a unique nonce value for each request. Specifying a nonce makes a modern browser ignore 'unsafe-inline' which could still be set for older browsers without nonce support.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.ScriptSourceDirectiveBuilder.ReportSample
member.summary: Requires a sample of the violating code to be included in the violation report
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.ScriptSourceElemDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The script-src-elem directive specifies valid sources for JavaScript <script> elements.
This directive only specifies valid sources in <script> elements (both script requests and blocks). It does not apply to other JavaScript sources that can trigger script execution, such as inline script event handlers (onclick), script execution methods gated on the "unsafe-eval" check, and XSLT stylesheets. (Valid sources can be specified for all JavaScript script sources using script-src, or just for inline script handlers using script-src-attr.)

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.StyleSourceAttrDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The style-src-attr directive specifies valid sources for inline styles applied to individual DOM elements.
The directive does not set valid sources for <style> elements and <link> elements with rel="stylesheet". These are set using style-src-elem (and valid sources for all styles may be set with style-src).

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.StyleSourceElemDirectiveBuilder.WithNonce
member.summary: A whitelist for specific inline scripts using a cryptographic nonce (number used once). The server generates a unique nonce value for each request. Specifying a nonce makes a modern browser ignore 'unsafe-inline' which could still be set for older browsers without nonce support.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.StyleSourceElemDirectiveBuilder.ReportSample
member.summary: Requires a sample of the violating code to be included in the violation report
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.TrustedTypesDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The trusted-types directive instructs user agents to restrict the creation of Trusted Types policies - functions that build non-spoofable, typed values intended to be passed to DOM XSS sinks in place of strings.
Together with this allows authors to define rules guarding writing values to the DOM and thus reducing the DOM XSS attack surface to small, isolated parts of the web application codebase, facilitating their monitoring and code review. This directive declares an allowlist of trusted type policy names created with trustedTypes.createPolicy from Trusted Types API.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.TrustedTypesDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.TrustedTypesDirectiveBuilder.Policies
member.summary: The policies to apply.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.TrustedTypesDirectiveBuilder.AllowDuplicates
member.summary: Allows for creating policies with a name that was already used.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.TrustedTypesDirectiveBuilder.None
member.summary: Disallows creating any Trusted Type policy (same as not specifying any policyName). Ignored if additional policies are provided.
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.TrustedTypesDirectiveBuilder.Default
member.summary: Allows creating the default policy
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.TrustedTypesDirectiveBuilder.Any
member.summary: Allows creating any Trusted Type policy (same as not specifying any policyName).
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.TrustedTypesDirectiveBuilder.AllowPolicy(System.String)
member.summary: Adds a policy name to the allow-list for trusted type policies. A valid policy name consists only of alphanumeric characters, or one of -#=_/@.%
member.returns: The CSP builder for method chaining
member.param: The policy name to add

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.ContentSecurityPolicy.UpgradeInsecureRequestsDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The upgrade-insecure-requests directive instructs user agents to treat all of a site's insecure URLs (those served over HTTP) as though they have been replaced with secure URLs (those served over HTTPS). This directive is intended for web sites with large numbers of insecure legacy URLs that need to be rewritten.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.CrossOriginPolicies.EmbedderPolicy.CredentiallessDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: no-cors cross-origin requests are sent without credentials. In particular, it means Cookies are omitted from the request, and ignored from the response. The responses are allowed without an explicit permission via the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header. Navigate responses behave similarly as the require-corp mode: They require Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy response header. From: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy#directives

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.CrossOriginPolicies.EmbedderPolicy.RequireCorpDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: A document can only load resources from the same origin, or resources explicitly marked as loadable from another origin. If a cross origin resource supports CORS, the crossorigin attribute or the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header must be used to load it without being blocked by COEP. From: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy#directives

From: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy#directives

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.FeaturePolicy.AccelerometerFeaturePolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the accelerometer sensor. If disabled then constructing of a Sensor-based interface object will throw a SecurityError. The events are not fired. If an interface (or an event) requires access to multiple sensors of different types than each of the corresponding sensor features must be allowed in order to use the interface.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.FeaturePolicy.AmbientLightSensorFeaturePolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the ambient light sensor sensor. If disabled then constructing of a Sensor-based interface object will throw a SecurityError. The events are not fired. If an interface (or an event) requires access to multiple sensors of different types than each of the corresponding sensor features must be allowed in order to use the interface.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.FeaturePolicy.AutoplayFeaturePolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: Controls access to autoplay of media requested through the HTMLMediaElement interface. If disabled in a document, then calls to play() without a user gesture will reject the promise with a NotAllowedError DOMException object as its parameter. The autoplay attribute will be ignored.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.AttributionReportingPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The HTTP Permissions-Policy header attribution-reporting directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Attribution Reporting API. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks the use of this feature:
Background attributionsrc requests won't be made.
The XMLHttpRequest.setAttributionReporting() method will throw an exception when called.
The attributionReporting option, when included on a fetch() call, will cause it to throw an exception.
Registration headers (Attribution-Reporting-Register-Source and Attribution-Reporting-Register-Trigger) in HTTP responses on associated documents will be ignored.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.AttributionReportingPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.AutoplayPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: Controls access to autoplay of media requested through the HTMLMediaElement interface. If disabled in a document, then calls to play() without a user gesture will reject the promise with a NotAllowedError DOMException object as its parameter. The autoplay attribute will be ignored.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.AutoplayPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.BluetoothPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The HTTP Permissions-Policy header bluetooth directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Web Bluetooth API. Specifically, where a defined policy disallows use of this feature, the methods of the Bluetooth object returned by Navigator.bluetooth, will block access:
Bluetooth.getAvailability() will always fulfill its returned Promise with a value of false.
Bluetooth.getDevices() will reject its returned Promise with a SecurityError DOMException.
Bluetooth.requestDevice() will reject its returned Promise with a SecurityError DOMException.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.BluetoothPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.DisplayCapturePermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header display-capture directive controls whether or not the document is permitted to use Screen Capture API, that is, getDisplayMedia() to capture the screen's contents. If display-capture is disabled in a document, the document will not be able to initiate screen capture via getDisplayMedia() and will throw a NotAllowedError exception.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.HidPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header hid directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the WebHID API to connect to uncommon or exotic human interface devices such as alternative keyboards or gamepads. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks WebHID usage, the Navigator.hid property will not be available.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.HidPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.IdentityCredentialsGetPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header identity-credentials-get directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Federated Credential Management API (FedCM), and more specifically the navigator.credentials.get() method with an identity option. Where this policy forbids use of the API, the Promise returned by the get() call will reject with a NotAllowedError DOMException.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.IdentityCredentialsGetPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.IdleDetectionPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header idle-detection directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Idle Detection API to detect when users are interacting with their devices, for example to report "available"/"away" status in chat applications. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, IdleDetector.start() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type NotAllowedError.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.IdleDetectionPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.LocalFontsPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header local-fonts directive controls whether the current document is allowed to gather data on the user's locally-installed fonts via the Window.queryLocalFonts() method. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, Promises returned by Window.queryLocalFonts() will reject with a DOMException of type SecurityError.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.LocalFontsPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.OtpCredentialsPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header otp-credentials directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the WebOTP API to request a one-time password (OTP) from a specially-formatted SMS message sent by the app's server, i.e., via navigator.credentials.get({otp: ..., ...}). Specifically, where a defined policy blocks the use of this feature, the Promise returned by navigator.credentials.get({otp}) will reject with a SecurityError DOMException.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.PublickeyCredentialsCreatePermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header publickey-credentials-create directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Web Authentication API to create new WebAuthn credentials, i.e., via navigator.credentials.create({publicKey}). Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, the Promise returned by navigator.credentials.create({publicKey}) will reject with a NotAllowedError DOMException. If the method is called cross-origin. the Promise will also reject with a NotAllowedError if the feature is granted by allow= on an iframe and the frame does not also have Transient activation.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.PublickeyCredentialsCreatePermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.PublickeyCredentialsGetPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header publickey-credentials-get directive controls whether the current document is allowed to access the Web Authentication API to retrieve public-key credentials, i.e., via navigator.credentials.get({publicKey}). Specifically, where a defined policy blocks the use of this feature, the Promise returned by navigator.credentials.get({publicKey}) will reject with a NotAllowedError DOMException.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.PublickeyCredentialsGetPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.ScreenWakeLockPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header screen-wake-lock directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use Screen Wake Lock API to indicate that the device should not dim or turn off the screen. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, WakeLock.request() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type NotAllowedError.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.ScreenWakeLockPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.SerialPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header serial directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Web Serial API to communicate with serial devices, either directly connected via a serial port, or via USB or Bluetooth devices emulating a serial port. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, Serial.requestPort() and Serial.getPorts() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type SecurityError.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.SerialPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.SpeakerPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: Controls access to audio output devices requested through the NavigatorUserMedia interface. If disabled then calls to getUserMedia() will not grant access to audio output devices in that document.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.SpeakerPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.SpeakerSelectionPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The HTTP Permissions-Policy header speaker-selection directive controls whether the current document is allowed to enumerate and select audio output devices (speakers, headphones, and so on). Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature:
MediaDevices.enumerateDevices() won't return devices of type audio output.
MediaDevices.selectAudioOutput() won't display the popup for selecting an audio output, and the returned Promise will reject with a DOMException of type NotAllowedError.
HTMLMediaElement.setSinkId() and AudioContext.setSinkId() will throw a NotAllowedError if called for an audio output.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.SpeakerSelectionPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.StorageAccessPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header storage-access directive controls whether a document loaded in a third-party context (i.e. embedded in an iframe) is allowed to use the Storage Access API to request access to unpartitioned cookies. This is relevant to user agents that by default block access to unpartitioned cookies by sites loaded in a third-party context to improve privacy (for example, to prevent tracking). Specifically, where a defined policy blocks use of this feature, Document.requestStorageAccess() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type NotAllowedError.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.StorageAccessPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.SynchronousXhrPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use synchronous XMLHttpRequest transfers. If disabled in a document, then calls to send() on XMLHttpRequest objects will throw a NetworkError.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.SynchronousXhrPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.UsbPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the WebUSB API. If disabled then calls to getDevices() should return a promise which rejects with a SecurityError DOMException.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.UsbPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.VRPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: Controls whether the current document is allowed to use the WebVR API. If disabled then calls to getVRDisplays() should return a promise which rejects with a SecurityError DOMException.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.VRPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.WebSharePermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary: The HTTP Permissions-Policy header web-share directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the Navigator.share() method of the Web Share API to share text, links, images, and other content to arbitrary destinations of the user's choice. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks usage of this feature, Navigator.share() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type NotAllowedError.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.WebSharePermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.WindowManagementPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The HTTP Permissions-Policy header window-management directive controls whether or not the current document is allowed to use the Window Management API to manage windows on multiple displays. Where this policy forbids use of the API:
The Promise returned by the Window.getScreenDetails() method will reject with a NotAllowedError exception.
The Window.screen.isExtended property will always return false.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.WindowManagementPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.XrSpatialTrackingPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder
member.summary:
The HTTP Permissions-Policy header xr-spatial-tracking directive controls whether the current document is allowed to use the WebXR Device API. Specifically, where a defined policy blocks usage of this feature:
navigator.xr.isSessionSupported() and navigator.xr.requestSession() calls will return a Promise that rejects with a DOMException of type SecurityError.
devicechange events are not fired on the navigator.xr object.

member.name: M:NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders.Headers.PermissionsPolicy.XrSpatialTrackingPermissionsPolicyDirectiveBuilder.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
